title: TV Show Says No to Old Joke Book
summary: A TV show called The Repair Shop decided not to fix a joke book that belonged to Bob Monkhouse, a famous comedian who passed away. The book was brought to the show by his daughter and writing partner. The people making the show looked at the book and found many jokes that were not right for their program. They told the family and gave the book back without filming it. Bob Monkhouse was a well-known TV star for many years before he died in 2003. His joke books have a history, like being stolen once. The show usually fixes old items, including things from celebrities, but this time they chose not to because of the jokes.

keywords.explanation: a book filled with funny stories or lines
a person who tells jokes and makes people laugh
recording with a camera for TV
people related to each other, like parents and children

questions.question: What did The Repair Shop decide about Bob Monkhouse's joke book?
Who brought the joke book to the show?
Why did the show not film the joke book?
What did the show do with the book after deciding not to fix it?
What does The Repair Shop usually do?

background_read: The Repair Shop is a BBC TV show where experts fix old and broken items for people. Bob Monkhouse was a popular British comedian who appeared on TV for many years. Joke books are collections of jokes that comedians use to make people laugh. Sometimes, jokes can be inappropriate, meaning they are not suitable for everyone, especially on family-friendly shows like The Repair Shop.
Article_Structure: WHO: Bob Monkhouse, his daughter, writing partner, and The Repair Shop team
WHAT: The Repair Shop rejected fixing a joke book because the jokes were inappropriate
WHERE: On the TV show The Repair Shop
WHY: To keep the program family-friendly and avoid inappropriate content

perspectives.description: The show wanted to protect its image by not including jokes that might offend viewers.
